review_focus: Phase 6n+6o → Phase 7 absorption Session 1 D.3 GATE 2 paired-splash. Verification of new 'Substrate-class context' paragraph (sec:discussion lines 284-317) inserted after Jacobson-Koike contrast; Verlinde-vs-Jacobson distinction preservation; cross-bundle consistency with D3 §17.5 (sec:sakharov-horizon-crooks).

sentences.quote: The Bardeen--Carter--Hawking (BCH) four laws of black-hole mechanics admit two structurally distinct realizations on an emergent-gravity ADW substrate, separated by a critical mass $\Mc(\aADW, \Ladw, \Nf) = \Nf \Ladw / (12\pi \aADW)$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bardeen--carter--hawking (bch) four laws of black-hole mechanics admit two structurally distinct realizations on an emergent-gravity adw substrate, separated by a critical mass $\mc(\aadw, \ladw, \nf) = \nf \ladw / (12\pi \aadw)$.
sentences.agent_notes: Critical-mass formula M_c = N_f Λ_UV / (12π α_ADW) traces to BHThermodynamicsFourLaws.lean (regime_partition_criterion + M_c_pos). Project-original formula explicitly disclosed; verified live in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: Above $\Mc$ the BH follows the textbook Hawking 1975 profile $\Thawk = \hbar/(8\pi M)$~\cite{Hawking1975CMP}: $dM/dt < 0$, $d\Thawk/dt > 0$ (heats as it evaporates), finite evaporation time $t_{\rm evap} \sim M^3$.
sentences.quote_normalized: above $\mc$ the bh follows the textbook hawking 1975 profile $\thawk = \hbar/(8\pi m)$~: $dm/dt < 0$, $d\thawk/dt > 0$ (heats as it evaporates), finite evaporation time $t_{\rm evap} \sim m^3$.
sentences.agent_notes: Hawking1975CMP in CITATION_REGISTRY (line 3465) with primary-source cache. Schwarzschild profile T_H = 1/(8πM), heating sign claim formalized in T_H_schwarzschild_strict_decreasing.

sentences.quote: Below $\Mc$ the BH follows the BEC-acoustic backreaction profile of Balbinot, Fagnocchi, Fabbri, Procopio (PRD~\textbf{71}, 064019)~\cite{Balbinot2005PRD}: linear-in-$t$ cooling with extrapolation $t \sim 1/T^3$, so $\Thawk \to 0$ in \emph{infinite} time --- the analog of near-extremal Reissner--Nordstr\"om.
sentences.quote_normalized: below $\mc$ the bh follows the bec-acoustic backreaction profile of balbinot, fagnocchi, fabbri, procopio (prd~71, 064019)~: linear-in-$t$ cooling with extrapolation $t \sim 1/t^3$, so $\thawk \to 0$ in infinite time --- the analog of near-extremal reissner--nordstr\"om.
sentences.agent_notes: Balbinot2005PRD in CITATION_REGISTRY (line 3439) with primary-source PDF. Decreasing-T claim formalized in T_H_acoustic_evolution_strict_decreasing. Per claims-reviewer-bundle-prompts.md L3 anchor: Balbinot is correct primary anchor (NOT 3He-A heating); satisfied.

sentences.quote: The genuine regime partition is the \emph{sign-flip of $d\Thawk/dt$ during Hawking-radiation evaporation}: positive in the Schwarzschild branch, negative in the BEC-acoustic branch.
sentences.quote_normalized: the genuine regime partition is the sign-flip of $d\thawk/dt$ during hawking-radiation evaporation: positive in the schwarzschild branch, negative in the bec-acoustic branch.
sentences.agent_notes: Sign-flip headline claim is the bundle's load-bearing theorem regime_partition_criterion (line 467 in BHThermodynamicsFourLaws.lean).

sentences.quote: We formalize both regimes in \texttt{BHThermodynamicsFourLaws.lean} (\bhThermoTotal{} theorems and lemmas; zero \texttt{sorry}; zero new axioms) and ship the classification, the regime-partition criterion, and the four-laws bundle in tracked-hypothesis mode (Outcome-3 per the project's discipline for non-derived structural identifications).
sentences.quote_normalized: we formalize both regimes in bhthermodynamicsfourlaws.lean (\bhthermototal{} theorems and lemmas; zero sorry; zero new axioms) and ship the classification, the regime-partition criterion, and the four-laws bundle in tracked-hypothesis mode (outcome-3 per the project's discipline for non-derived structural identifications).

sentences.quote: The full second law uses Glorioso--Liu SK-EFT entropy-current monotonicity~\cite{GloriosoLiu2018,CrossleyGloriosoLiu2017,GloriosoCrossleyLiu2017} without invoking pointwise NEC.
sentences.quote_normalized: the full second law uses glorioso--liu sk-eft entropy-current monotonicity~ without invoking pointwise nec.
sentences.agent_notes: All three Glorioso-Liu citations in CITATION_REGISTRY (lines 730, 3093, 3548). Phase 6n W2a Glorioso-Liu axiomatic skeleton lifted into L3 §3.

sentences.quote: The substrate-response coefficient ansatz $\dADW = (\aADW - 1)\Ladw$ vanishes at $\aADW = 1$ (the bare Sakharov--Adler limit) and provides four falsifier theorems for any candidate ADW substrate.
sentences.quote_normalized: the substrate-response coefficient ansatz $\dadw = (\aadw - 1)\ladw$ vanishes at $\aadw = 1$ (the bare sakharov--adler limit) and provides four falsifier theorems for any candidate adw substrate.

sentences.quote: The Bardeen--Carter--Hawking four laws codify a thermodynamic structure on stationary BH solutions of classical general relativity.
sentences.quote_normalized: the bardeen--carter--hawking four laws codify a thermodynamic structure on stationary bh solutions of classical general relativity.
sentences.agent_notes: Background framing; no extractable claim.

sentences.quote: On a Schwarzschild background, quantum field theory endows the surface gravity $\kappa$ with a thermal interpretation as the Hawking temperature $\Thawk = \hbar\kappa/(2\pi)$~\cite{Hawking1975CMP}, and the resulting heat-capacity sign $C(M) = (dM/d\Thawk)$ is \emph{negative}: the BH \emph{heats} as it loses mass to Hawking evaporation, with finite evaporation time $t_{\rm evap} \propto M_0^3$.
sentences.quote_normalized: on a schwarzschild background, quantum field theory endows the surface gravity $\kappa$ with a thermal interpretation as the hawking temperature $\thawk = \hbar\kappa/(2\pi)$~, and the resulting heat-capacity sign $c(m) = (dm/d\thawk)$ is negative: the bh heats as it loses mass to hawking evaporation, with finite evaporation time $t_{\rm evap} \propto m_0^3$.
sentences.agent_notes: Standard Hawking-temperature formula; correctly cited.

sentences.quote: Analog and emergent-gravity systems offer a structurally different evaporation pattern.
sentences.quote_normalized: analog and emergent-gravity systems offer a structurally different evaporation pattern.
sentences.agent_notes: Topic-shift transition.

sentences.quote: In the BEC-acoustic backreaction analysis of Balbinot, Fagnocchi, Fabbri, Procopio~\cite{Balbinot2005PRD}, backreaction of thermally radiated phonons on the fluid background \emph{shrinks} the sonic horizon and \emph{decreases} $\Thawk$ in time, with linear-in-$t$ leading order [Eq.~\eqref{eq:Tsonic}].
sentences.quote_normalized: in the bec-acoustic backreaction analysis of balbinot, fagnocchi, fabbri, procopio~, backreaction of thermally radiated phonons on the fluid background shrinks the sonic horizon and decreases $\thawk$ in time, with linear-in-$t$ leading order.
sentences.agent_notes: Balbinot 2005 leading-order linear-in-t profile. Eq. eq:Tsonic shows the 563/720π coefficient — consistent with Balbinot et al. (verified against backreaction.py rational-cooling model).

sentences.quote: Reference~\cite{Balbinot2005PRD} itself contrasts this regime with the moving-domain-wall analog in $^{3}$He-A studied by Jacobson--Koike~\cite{JacobsonKoike2002, JacobsonVolovik1998}, which has \emph{non}-vanishing end-temperature of the evaporation process and therefore behaves more like Schwarzschild.
sentences.quote_normalized: reference~ itself contrasts this regime with the moving-domain-wall analog in $^{3}$he-a studied by jacobson--koike~, which has non-vanishing end-temperature of the evaporation process and therefore behaves more like schwarzschild.
sentences.agent_notes: All three citations verified in registry. Per L3 anchors (claims-reviewer-bundle-prompts.md), Jacobson-Koike correctly used as CONTRAST (not the cooling anchor); satisfies feedback_deep_research_analog_conflation.md.

sentences.quote: This Letter formalizes both regimes within a single Lean module \texttt{BHThermodynamicsFourLaws.lean} and ships the sign-of-$d\Thawk/dt$ regime partition as the load-bearing correctness-push theorem.
sentences.quote_normalized: this letter formalizes both regimes within a single lean module bhthermodynamicsfourlaws.lean and ships the sign-of-$d\thawk/dt$ regime partition as the load-bearing correctness-push theorem.
sentences.agent_notes: Module + load-bearing theorem present.

sentences.quote: We do \emph{not} attempt a single closed-form $\Thawk(M)$ that interpolates between regimes; we treat the existence of a critical mass $\Mc(\aADW, \Ladw, \Nf)$ separating them as a tracked-hypothesis bundle $H_{\rm RegimePartition}$ with each branch's sign claim grounded in its primary literature.
sentences.quote_normalized: we do not attempt a single closed-form $\thawk(m)$ that interpolates between regimes; we treat the existence of a critical mass $\mc(\aadw, \ladw, \nf)$ separating them as a tracked-hypothesis bundle $h_{\rm regimepartition}$ with each branch's sign claim grounded in its primary literature.
sentences.agent_notes: Honest scope-disclaimer; tracked-hypothesis Outcome-3 mode.

sentences.quote: The zeroth law ($\kappa$ constant on the horizon) and first law ($dM = (\kappa/8\pi) dA + \Omega\,dJ + \Phi\,dQ$) are substrate-agnostic.
sentences.quote_normalized: the zeroth law ($\kappa$ constant on the horizon) and first law ($dm = (\kappa/8\pi) da + \omega\,dj + \phi\,dq$) are substrate-agnostic.

sentences.quote: The second law (entropy non-decrease) is formalized via Glorioso--Liu SK-EFT entropy-current monotonicity~\cite{GloriosoLiu2018,CrossleyGloriosoLiu2017,GloriosoCrossleyLiu2017} on the open Schwinger--Keldysh action, without invoking pointwise NEC; this is the load-bearing substrate-class generalization compared to the standard Bekenstein--Hawking-on-area-law treatment.
sentences.quote_normalized: the second law (entropy non-decrease) is formalized via glorioso--liu sk-eft entropy-current monotonicity~ on the open schwinger--keldysh action, without invoking pointwise nec; this is the load-bearing substrate-class generalization compared to the standard bekenstein--hawking-on-area-law treatment.
sentences.agent_notes: Three Glorioso-Liu citations all in registry. Substrate-class generalization claim is appropriately scoped vs. standard BH-area-law.

sentences.quote: The third law (Israel's strong form: $\kappa \to 0$ attainable only at infinite affine time; equivalently, no extremal-horizon formation in finite proper time) holds in both branches under the regime-specific asymptotics: the BEC-acoustic branch reaches the $\Thawk \to 0$ extremality only at $t \to \infty$ (Balbinot 2005 $t \sim 1/T^3$ extrapolation), and the Schwarzschild branch evaporates completely at finite proper time without ever crossing the $\kappa \to 0$ locus along the evaporation trajectory.
sentences.quote_normalized: the third law (israel's strong form: $\kappa \to 0$ attainable only at infinite affine time; equivalently, no extremal-horizon formation in finite proper time) holds in both branches under the regime-specific asymptotics: the bec-acoustic branch reaches the $\thawk \to 0$ extremality only at $t \to \infty$ (balbinot 2005 $t \sim 1/t^3$ extrapolation), and the schwarzschild branch evaporates completely at finite proper time without ever crossing the $\kappa \to 0$ locus along the evaporation trajectory.
sentences.agent_notes: Israel third-law strong form correctly attributed; both branches' regime-specific behaviors stated correctly per Balbinot 2005 (t~1/T^3 → infinite-time approach) and standard Schwarzschild evaporation.

sentences.quote: Recent third-law revisions for extremal BPS BHs~\cite{KehleUnger2022ThirdLaw,Reall2024ThirdLawBPS} do not bear on the Schwarzschild branch; their applicability to the ADW-extremality branch is a Phase-6 cross-bridge open question.
sentences.quote_normalized: recent third-law revisions for extremal bps bhs~ do not bear on the schwarzschild branch; their applicability to the adw-extremality branch is a phase-6 cross-bridge open question.
sentences.agent_notes: Both Kehle-Unger 2022 and Reall 2024 in registry; honest open-question disclosure.

sentences.quote: Three claims are project-original and explicitly flagged: (1) the critical-mass functional form $\Mc = \Nf \Ladw / (12\pi \aADW)$, no published ADW derivation exists; (2) the BEC-acoustic exponential approximation Eq.~\eqref{eq:Tacoustic} as the load-bearing analytic anchor for the strict-decreasing claim, an analytic compression of the coupled-ODE system in~\cite{Balbinot2005PRD}; (3) the substrate-response coefficient $\dADW = (\aADW - 1)\Ladw$ as a unified four-falsifier witness across the bundle's degrees of freedom.
sentences.quote_normalized: three claims are project-original and explicitly flagged: (1) the critical-mass functional form $\mc = \nf \ladw / (12\pi \aadw)$, no published adw derivation exists; (2) the bec-acoustic exponential approximation eq.~ as the load-bearing analytic anchor for the strict-decreasing claim, an analytic compression of the coupled-ode system in~; (3) the substrate-response coefficient $\dadw = (\aadw - 1)\ladw$ as a unified four-falsifier witness across the bundle's degrees of freedom.
sentences.agent_notes: Honest novelty disclosure of three project-original elements.

sentences.quote: We do \emph{not} derive a single closed-form $\Thawk(M)$ interpolating the regimes; the boundary $M = \Mc$ is a discrete-classification boundary in the inductive type, not a smooth crossover.
sentences.quote_normalized: we do not derive a single closed-form $\thawk(m)$ interpolating the regimes; the boundary $m = \mc$ is a discrete-classification boundary in the inductive type, not a smooth crossover.
sentences.agent_notes: Honest scope-disclaimer.

sentences.quote: We do \emph{not} prove that the Glorioso--Liu second law operates strictly identically in the two regimes; entropy-current monotonicity holds in each regime under its respective hypothesis bundle, but the cross-regime entropy matching at the boundary is a Phase-6 cross-bridge.
sentences.quote_normalized: we do not prove that the glorioso--liu second law operates strictly identically in the two regimes; entropy-current monotonicity holds in each regime under its respective hypothesis bundle, but the cross-regime entropy matching at the boundary is a phase-6 cross-bridge.
sentences.agent_notes: Honest open-question disclosure.

sentences.quote: The moving-domain-wall analog in $^{3}$He-A studied by Jacobson--Koike~\cite{JacobsonKoike2002} gives a heating profile ($\Thawk(v) = \Thawk^{(0)}(1 - v^2/c_\perp^2)$ monotonically decreasing in $v$, but the back-reaction \emph{slows} the soliton $dv/dt < 0$, so $\Thawk$ \emph{increases} in time).
sentences.quote_normalized: the moving-domain-wall analog in $^{3}$he-a studied by jacobson--koike~ gives a heating profile ($\thawk(v) = \thawk^{(0)}(1 - v^2/c_\perp^2)$ monotonically decreasing in $v$, but the back-reaction slows the soliton $dv/dt < 0$, so $\thawk$ increases in time).
sentences.agent_notes: Jacobson-Koike 2002 cited correctly as 3He-A heating contrast.

sentences.quote: This regime is structurally similar to Schwarzschild, not to near-extremal Reissner--Nordstr\"om.
sentences.quote_normalized: this regime is structurally similar to schwarzschild, not to near-extremal reissner--nordstr\"om.
sentences.agent_notes: Honest classification of 3He-A as Schwarzschild-like, not Reissner-Nordström-like (preserves L3-anchor discipline against analog-conflation).

sentences.quote: The $^{3}$He-A cell and the BEC-acoustic substrate are members of complementary Sakharov-criterion classes.
sentences.quote_normalized: the $^{3}$he-a cell and the bec-acoustic substrate are members of complementary sakharov-criterion classes.

Cross-bundle consistent with D3 §17.5 (sec:sakharov-horizon-crooks lines 1310-1349).

sentences.quote: The Volovik--Jannes formalisation in the program's \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean} (Phase 6m Track C) shows that $^{3}$He-A satisfies all four Sakharov conditions (\texttt{JTGR7}: emergent IR Lorentz, universal coupling, induced Newton constant, and the $\Lambda_{\mathrm{eff}} = \Lambda_{\mathrm{HK}}$ equilibrium identity), while the Finazzi--Liberati--Sindoni acoustic-BEC violates condition (ii) (universal coupling: depletion-sector atoms do not propagate on the BEC effective metric; \texttt{JTGR8}).
sentences.quote_normalized: the volovik--jannes formalisation in the program's lean/skefthawking/jacobsonthermogrdarkenergy.lean (phase 6m track c) shows that $^{3}$he-a satisfies all four sakharov conditions (jtgr7: emergent ir lorentz, universal coupling, induced newton constant, and the $\lambda_{eff} = \lambda_{hk}$ equilibrium identity), while the finazzi--liberati--sindoni acoustic-bec violates condition (ii) (universal coupling: depletion-sector atoms do not propagate on the bec effective metric; jtgr8).
sentences.agent_notes: JTGR7 and JTGR8 are docstring-tags (line 341, 346 of JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy.lean) for theorems volovik_jannes_he3a_satisfies_sakharov_criterion (line 343) and finazzi_liberati_sindoni_bec_violates_universal_coupling (line 355). Both theorems verified live. \texttt{JTGR7}/\texttt{JTGR8} references are project-internal docstring-aliases — not theorem names — so not a Class TN finding. Substrate-class claim accurately matches the Lean module content.

sentences.quote: The Phase 6n unification (\texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/CrooksAnalogHawking/SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean}) shows that the four Sakharov conditions are biconditional with the horizon-Crooks detailed-balance identity at the local Rindler horizon (\texttt{horizonCrooks\_substrate\_partition}; the companion deep paper~\cite{Roehm2026EmergentGravity} carries the full subsection).
sentences.quote_normalized: the phase 6n unification (lean/skefthawking/crooksanaloghawking/sakharovhorizoncrooks.lean) shows that the four sakharov conditions are biconditional with the horizon-crooks detailed-balance identity at the local rindler horizon (horizoncrooks\_substrate\_partition; the companion deep paper~ carries the full subsection).
sentences.agent_notes: Theorem horizonCrooks_substrate_partition VERIFIED present at line 187 of SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean (helium3A jacobsonConsistent ∧ ¬flsBEC jacobsonConsistent — the substrate partition theorem). Module SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean exists with HorizonCrooksSubstrate predicate + jacobsonConsistent def. HOWEVER: WARN — Citation Roehm2026EmergentGravity is NOT in CITATION_REGISTRY (citations.py only has Roehm2026Strategy and Roehm2026Modular, lines 7429/7440). The bibitem (lines 408-411 of L3 paper_draft.tex) declares it as 'in preparation (D3 deep companion to L3, 2026)'. Per project convention, in-prep self-references are acceptable in bibitems but should be added to CITATION_REGISTRY. RECOMMEND: add Roehm2026EmergentGravity entry to citations.py (in-prep status) before L3 submission. Caveat that the bicondtional claim 'four Sakharov conditions ↔ horizon-Crooks' is broader than what horizonCrooks_substrate_partition strictly proves: that theorem is a 2-witness partition (³He-A passes, FLS BEC fails). The biconditional itself lives across SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean's HorizonCrooksSubstrate.jacobsonConsistent definition (which BUNDLES SakharovConditions ∧ HorizonDetailedBalance) plus the partition theorem. Prose claim is consistent with how Lean module bundles them.

sentences.quote: The regime partition formalised here (sign-flip of $d\Thawk/dt$) is \emph{not} directly equivalent to Sakharov-criterion satisfaction: the BEC-acoustic ($M < M_c$) cooling regime persists \emph{despite} Sakharov-criterion failure on the FLS substrate, because the regime partition is a thermodynamic-evolution claim about $\Thawk(t)$ rather than a substrate-induced-gravity claim.
sentences.quote_normalized: the regime partition formalised here (sign-flip of $d\thawk/dt$) is not directly equivalent to sakharov-criterion satisfaction: the bec-acoustic ($m < m_c$) cooling regime persists despite sakharov-criterion failure on the fls substrate, because the regime partition is a thermodynamic-evolution claim about $\thawk(t)$ rather than a substrate-induced-gravity claim.
sentences.agent_notes: Substantive non-overclaim: sharply distinguishes thermodynamic-evolution (regime partition / sign-flip) from substrate-induced-gravity (Sakharov/horizon-Crooks). This is exactly the discrimination flagged in the Session-1 brief — the L3 paragraph correctly disclaims that the two classifications are independent program axes, NOT equivalent. Honest scope-discipline.

sentences.quote: The two classifications (Sakharov-criterion + regime partition) are independent program axes; horizon-Crooks unifies them only at the substrate-induced-gravity layer (the Jacobson 1995 equilibrium-Clausius reading~\cite{JacobsonEFE1995} of entropic gravity), not at the thermodynamic-evolution layer of the regime classifier.
sentences.quote_normalized: the two classifications (sakharov-criterion + regime partition) are independent program axes; horizon-crooks unifies them only at the substrate-induced-gravity layer (the jacobson 1995 equilibrium-clausius reading~ of entropic gravity), not at the thermodynamic-evolution layer of the regime classifier.
sentences.agent_notes: JacobsonEFE1995 in CITATION_REGISTRY (line 3982, gr-qc/9504004 — Phys.Rev.Lett. 75, 1260 (1995)) with primary-source PDF cache. Verlinde-vs-Jacobson distinction PRESERVED at this claim site: explicitly limits horizon-Crooks unification to the Jacobson 1995 equilibrium-Clausius reading (NOT the Verlinde entropic-force reading). Mirrors D3 §17.5 line 1352-1354 (sec:sakharov-horizon-crooks 'Verlinde-vs-Jacobson distinction' paragraph) — cross-bundle consistent.

sentences.quote: The fluctuation-theorem reading of the unification is consistent only with the equilibrium-Clausius derivation of the Einstein equation; it does not entail Verlinde-class entropic-force gravity, which the program separately disfavours at $\geq 5\sigma$ across eight independent observational checks (Phase 6m Track B).
